claims.claim: Bento layouts with more than 12-15 visible cards lose their organizational advantage; an 8-card vs 16-card test showed the 8-card version had 31% lower time-to-target-action.
claims.quote: bento layouts with more than 12-15 visible cards lose their organizational advantage. We tested 8-card vs 16-card layouts and the 8-card version had 31% lower time-to-target-action.

claims.claim: Bento grids fail on mobile because the visual hierarchy collapses into a stack of identical-width blocks.
claims.quote: They fail on mobile... The hierarchy collapses into a stack of identical-width blocks.

claims.claim: Grid is the correct tool for card grids, dashboards, and bento grids, while flexbox is for component-level alignment and navigation.
claims.quote: Grid: 'page-level structure, card grids, dashboards, bento grids' Flexbox: 'component-level alignment, navigation'

claims.claim: An audited bento layout had a DOM order vs visual order mismatch — the featured card was seventh in the DOM but first visually — causing tab order to jump non-linearly; the CSS `reading-flow` property (Chrome 131+) resolves this.
claims.quote: featured card was placed first visually... but was the seventh element in the DOM. Tab order jumped from top-left to bottom-right and back.

claims.claim: CSS subgrid has full implementations in Chrome 117+, Safari 16+, Firefox 71+, and Edge 117+, but global adoption counting older installs still sits below 90% on caniuse.
claims.quote: Chrome 117+, Safari 16+, Firefox 71+, and Edge 117+ all have full implementations... counting older installs still sits below 90% on caniuse
